source.title: Fire Incident During Hot Work Operations - SM 130 A Facility

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - South Marsh Island Block 130

actions_taken: Fire extinguished with 30# dry chemical extinguisher
event.summary: On February 4, 2023, at approximately 1405 hours, a small fire occurred on the thief hatch of the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank during welding and cutting operations to install a PAT-300 Water Injection Pump. Sparks from cutting operations ignited hydrocarbon vapors venting from the thief hatch. Fire-retardant blankets failed to provide adequate protection. The fire was extinguished with no damage to equipment or injuries to personnel. Investigation revealed multiple failures including inadequate gas monitoring, improper lockout/tagout procedures, insufficient fire protection barriers, and failure to implement previously identified mitigations.

hazards.name: Flammable hydrocarbon vapors in oil tank

threats.name: Ignition source from hot work (welding/cutting sparks and slag)
Hydrocarbon vapor release from thief hatch during hot work
Hydrocarbon ingress from ABJ-320 Wet Oil Tank through common vent line

controls.name: Fire-retardant blankets between work area and oil tank
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent spark/slag contact with flammable vapors
controls.lod_basis: Physical protection system to prevent ignition

supporting_text: Fire-retardant blankets were hung between the PAT-300 Water Injection Pump and the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank which were approximately 7 feet apart
the blankets were not located in a way that protected the thief hatch
The flame ignited due to the fire-retardant blankets failing to provide sufficient protection allowing slag to contact combustible vapers venting from the thief hatch

controls.name: Gas monitoring near thief hatch during hot work
controls.barrier_role: Detection of flammable atmosphere before and during hot work
controls.lod_basis: Monitoring to detect hazardous conditions before work begins

supporting_text: The JSA for the operation contained checked off boxes labeled Hot Work which stated the following, 'A gas test has been completed', and 'Gas will be monitored continuously'. The boxes were checked off, but according to personnel interviewed, gas readings were taken near the PAT-300 Water Injection Pump but had not been taken near the thief hatch on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank prior to hot work commencing and/or during the hot work operation

During the interview process, it was determined that gas detector readings were taken near the Water Injection Pump where welding operations were taking place but had not been taken prior to hot work commencing and/or during the hot work operation near the thief hatch on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout of ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank inlet valves
controls.barrier_role: Energy isolation to prevent hydrocarbon flow into tank
controls.lod_basis: Primary isolation to prevent hazardous material entry

supporting_text: The JSA identified Energy Isolation and Lockout/Tagout but the lessee failed to lock or tag any valves associated with the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

The ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank was isolated by closing all inlet valves from the NBK-300 Bulk Oil Treater but failed to lock and tag the valves as per the Talos Lockout / Tagout procedure - 7.3.5 Isolation of Various Systems
Not follow directions of the Lockout /Tagout Procedure. No blind skillets were installed and the inlet and outlet valves were not locked and tagged on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

controls.name: Blind/skillet installation for positive isolation
controls.barrier_role: Positive physical isolation of hazardous systems
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent hydrocarbon entry

supporting_text: a single closed valve is not an acceptable isolation for potentially hazardous systems in the vicinity of the vessel entry and/or hot work, except when the facility/equipment is shut in and all pressures have been bled to atmospheric pressure. Insertion of blinds/skillets isolating the source is the preferred method
There were no blinds/skillets installed during the welding operation nor was the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank isolated properly as per the mentioned policy
No blind skillets were installed and the inlet and outlet valves were not locked and tagged on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout of ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank outlet valves
controls.barrier_role: Energy isolation to prevent hydrocarbon release
controls.lod_basis: Primary isolation to prevent hazardous material release

supporting_text: The ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank outlet valves were not locked or tagged as per the Talos Lockout / Tagout procedure - 7.3.5 Isolation of Various Systems during welding operations either
No blind skillets were installed and the inlet and outlet valves were not locked and tagged on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

controls.name: Tapped blind on thief hatch with vent hose to safe area
controls.barrier_role: Prevent vapor accumulation and direct vapors away from hot work area
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to manage vapor release

supporting_text: Prior to the start of construction operations at SM -130 A, construction supervision had previously identified a potential for hydrocarbon accumulation near the ABJ-310-Dry Oil Tank thief hatch. Mitigations were to include installation of a tapped blind installed on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank to replace the thief hatch and installing a hose to vent the hydrocarbon accumulation to a safe area. Neither of these mitigations were completed prior to the hot work commencing

Mitigations were to include installation of a tapped blind installed on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank to replace the thief hatch and installing a hose to vent the hydrocarbon accumulation to a safe area. Neither of these mitigations were completed prior to the hot work commencing

controls.name: Hot Work Permit system
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify safe conditions before hot work
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative verification of safe work conditions

supporting_text: The JSA for the operation contained checked off boxes labeled Hot Work which stated the following, 'I confirm flammable material have been removed or isolated', 'A gas test has been completed', and 'Gas will be monitored continuously'. The boxes were checked off, but according to personnel interviewed, gas readings were taken near the PAT-300 Water Injection Pump but had not been taken near the thief hatch

Not following directions listed in the Hot Work Permit. The Hot Work Permit asked if, combustible and flammable materials have been removed or covered with fire-retardant blankets in good condition and sparks associated with grinding or welding have been evaluated and contained utilizing metal shields, flame resistant guards or shields. The answers are checked off yes

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for hot work
controls.lod_basis: Primary planning tool to identify and control hazards

supporting_text: The JSA included not tarping properly to catch all flying debris, which was located in the Prepping Area for the Hot Work section in the JSA
The JSA identified Energy Isolation and Lockout/Tagout but the lessee failed to lock or tag any valves associated with the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

controls.name: Fire watch personnel
controls.barrier_role: Early detection and response to fire
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection and immediate response capability

supporting_text: As the welder began cutting the grating clips, the downstairs fire watch observed a flame coming from the thief hatch on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

There was a fire watch assigned on the west side of the well bay and a fire watch assigned on the production deck near the sump. Both mentioned areas were not in the immediate area where the work was being performed

controls.name: Fire extinguisher (30# dry chemical)
controls.lod_basis: Secondary response equipment for fire control

supporting_text: The fire was extinguished utilizing a 30# dry chemical extinguisher
There was no damage to the facility or injuries to personnel due to this incident

controls.name: Isolation of common vent line between ABJ-310 and ABJ-320 tanks
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hydrocarbon vapor transfer between tanks

supporting_text: During the BSEE investigation, it was discovered that the lessee failed to identify the potential for hydrocarbons to enter the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank from the ABJ-320 Wet Oil Tank through a common vent line. The ABJ-320 Wet Oil Tank was in service during welding operations
The lessee failed to identify the potential for hydrocarbons to enter the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank from the ABJ-320 Wet Oil Tank through a common vent line

controls.name: Metal shields or flame resistant guards for spark containment
controls.barrier_role: Physical containment of sparks and slag
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to contain ignition sources

supporting_text: The Hot Work Permit asked if, combustible and flammable materials have been removed or covered with fire-retardant blankets in good condition and sparks associated with grinding or welding have been evaluated and contained utilizing metal shields, flame resistant guards or shields. The answers are checked off yes. If adequate barriers would have been provided for the thief hatch and the sparks contained, the threat of a fire would have been mitigated to low or no risk
The Danos construction crew failed to provide adequate protection from sparks and slag when welding and cutting near the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
